entry.title: Re: Extensibility Approaches in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
entry.content:
@pradeep_tcs 

 

Whether a customer using SAP S/4HANA Cloud in the public or private edition would need an additional ABAP environment depends on their specific customization and extension requirements. In the public cloud edition, customer customizations are more controlled to ensure standardization, simplified lifecycle management, and ease of updates.

For complex custom development or extensions, we recommend utilizing the SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP) ABAP environment. This separation allows customers to create custom applications, services, and APIs without compromising the core system's standard update process and maintainability.

For further reference,
1. Extensibility | SAP Help Portal

2. SAP Extensibility Explorer for SAP S/4HANA Cloud

entry.title: Outbound/Inbound Delivery Creation from Transportation Management
entry.content:
Recently, a colleague approached me with a question about creating deliveries from a freight order in the context of order-based transportation planning. To be honest, I didn’t have an answer at the time because I wasn’t familiar with how deliveries are created directly from freight orders. I only knew that deliveries are typically created from freight orders where freight units are generated from purchase orders or sales orders.

This question got me thinking, and I decided to explore the process myself. I also thought it would be beneficial to write a blog post about it, as this is a common query that many consultants might encounter.

In some businesses, the customer’s logistics team begins their transportation planning and freight procurement after creating purchase orders or sales orders. Once the planning is complete, a freight forwarder is selected, and the freight order is finalized. At this point, there is often a need to process the delivery. SAP provides functionality that enables deliveries to be processed directly from freight orders.

We can use this process if you want to create a delivery proposal in Transportation Management (TM) and send it to SAP ERP, where the delivery is created. The delivery proposal is based on transportation planning results, such as consolidation and scheduling, while considering transportation-relevant information like resource availability and transportation durations. TM also has the capability to trigger delivery creation prior to transportation planning.

Users can initiate delivery creation either through a background report or interactively. TM can either send delivery proposals automatically to SAP ERP for delivery creation or allow users to review the proposals before sending them to SAP ERP.

 

Users can create delivery proposals for order-based transportation requirements by using a background function, such as running the program /SCMTMS/DLV_BATCH via transaction SE38. Users can specify selection criteria or utilize selection profiles. In either case, they can also define a delivery profile. The system then selects the relevant documents, generates delivery proposals, and automatically sends them to SAP ERP.

Users can create deliveries interactively using the app "Create Deliveries - Delivery Builder".

Delivery proposal creation is triggered for specific business documents after planning is completed in the Transportation Cockpit. Users select the business documents in the Transportation Cockpit, specify a delivery profile, and click the Create Delivery Proposals button.

Configuration:

A delivery profile must be created using the app "Delivery Profile", as it is mandatory for delivery creation from TM.

Additionally, the delivery profile can be assigned in the configuration of the freight order type.

The above information should help you configure and create delivery proposals or deliveries from TM.

Thanks & Regards,

Ranadeep Basak.

SAP Transportation Management LE (Logistics Execution)

entry.title: Re: How to solve CORS issue in SAPUI5 application
entry.content:
Hello guys,

I find the same error and the project work correctly before. I assume that the root is the auth function change. Redirect to SSO login page. Need to add the auth property in the ui5.yaml like below. If it can directly pass authorization can work. 

server:
  customMiddleware:
    - name: fiori-tools-proxy
      afterMiddleware: compression
      configuration:
        ignoreCertError: true,
        backend:
        - path: /sap
          url: <actual URL goes here>
          client: "100"
          auth: '<user>:<pwd>'
Reference:
https://www.npmjs.com/package/@sap/ux-ui5-tooling#2-proxy

entry.title: SAP BW 7.5 migration to new box
entry.content:
Hi All,

We are brainstorming ideas to upgrade current BW 7.0 system.

The case we want to study is we install SAP BW 7.5 in a new box and then migrate all objects from current BW 7.0 to BW 7.5. (not upgrading the BW 7.0 system)

1. Is this feasible? Will the BW objects in BW 7.0 work successfully in BW 7.5? While reading about upgrade in same system (BW 7.0 to BW 7.5), I noted that BW 7.0 objects are supported by BW 7.5 thus making a technical upgrade without performing functional upgrade.

2. Any risks listed for such a case?

3. what about data? Won't we need to setup delta flows anew in the new BW 7.5 system?

4. How do we collect all objects (considering all dependencies) to ensure successful migration from source BW 7.0 to BW 7.5? Maybe start with a BEx and get all dependent objects using "transport connection" . But does that ensure sequence? Will it safer to transport like below,

  1. Info Area /Info Object Catalogs/Info Objects :  All these can be bundled into 1 transport.
  2. DSO/Cube/Multi-provider : All these can be bundled into 1 transport.
  3. Datasources: Collect the view/table/Datasource incase of a view based datasource. Collect Datasource/Function-Group/Function Module/Table/View.
  4. Tranformations/DTP's - these can go together.
  5. Infopackages.
  6. Queries.

 

Thanks.

entry.title: Re: Architecting Email Approval without My Inbox for SAP Build Process Automation Workflows: Part 1
entry.content:
Hi @mohit_bansal3 and @Adwait_Fadnavis,

Thank you for sharing this innovative solution for offline email approvals.  This is a common request I have also come across from customers, and your approach provides a creative way to address this functionality limitation in SAP Build Process Automation.  I have a few questions and suggestions that might help further enhance the solution:

1) Decoupling Email Notification Generation:
In Step 2 Work Items Generation, it appears that each workflow must generate its own email notification for a task.  Have you considered decoupling this dependency by creating another background automation?  This automation could periodically query new work items via the SAP Build Process Automation Workflow API and trigger the approval emails, ensuring greater flexibility and centralisation.

2) Desktop Agent Dependency:
The Background Automation for response validation requires a Desktop Agent for bot execution, presumably to leverage Automation SDKs like the Google Workspace SDK.  While this simplifies processing incoming emails, it introduces additional maintenance, cost, and potential points of failure.  Have you explored implementing this as a Business Process instead?  Using native Google APIs via Actions could enable a fully cloud-based, end-to-end solution with reduced dependencies.

3) Email Validation and Security:
How is the approver’s sender email validated to prevent spoofing?  Ensuring robust security for this process is crucial, and I’d be interested in understanding your approach to mitigating spoofing risks.

4) Improving the Approver User Experience:
Currently, the approver manually enters "ok" or "no" in the reply email, which might lead to errors.  Have you considered generating the notification email in HTML format with embedded Approve and Reject buttons?  These buttons could use mailto links to open a new email pre-populated with the recipient and response content.  Additionally, a more secure solution could involve embedding an encrypted token in the reply email.  This token could include the decision (approve/reject) and Workflow Instance ID for secure response validation.  Such an approach could be standardised in conjunction with the suggestion in Point 1 of decoupling email notification generation.

Your solution already addresses a significant customer need, and I hope these suggestions provide additional opportunities for refinement.  Thank you again for sharing this great work!

Regards,
Mustafa.

entry.title: GRC Tuesdays: Implications of AI in the field of Regulatory Compliance
entry.content:
As a software provider, when testing new concepts or collecting enhancement requests, we naturally turn to our most expert stakeholders: our customers and our partners.

And this is precisely what we did when we started working on our concept for Regulatory Intelligence: we listened to what customers wanted, what analysts suggested the market needed, and what partners recommended. Armed with these inputs, we put together a proposal and then opened a Customer Influence program – Innovation project regulatory intelligence for SAP GRC, to share our findings, detail our concept, and obtain direct feedback, validating or invalidating our assumptions and design on how Artificial Intelligence can facilitate this core compliance step.

But AI being AI with all its unknown at this stage, we also decided to pursue a parallel thread: asking an external party to look at this topic with fresh sets of eyes and tell us what they thought we could be missing in our analysis. To get an unbiased outside-in perspective.

We were lucky to have 3 students from the Lancaster University Management School in the UK elect to work on a report on this matter: Implications of AI in the field of Regulatory Compliance; A study into the benefits and risks of delegating the analysis of regulatory changes to an AI.

This short blog is a summary of their findings that I thought I would share as there are valuable insights for any organization wishing to adopt AI for regulatory intelligence.

 

Why do companies consider using AI for regulatory surveillance?

 

As I am sure you are aware, the number and complexity of regulations keep on increasing and organizations, especially SMEs, feel the heavy burden of tracking these ever-changing rules with limited resources.

Many compliance teams therefore wish to automate the process to reduce the manual workload and hope to use AI to:

  • Stay up to date with local, regional and global regulations
  • Identify required changes to internal controls to stay compliant with new requirements 
  • Find out the coverage of regulatory frameworks and leverage AI-generated suggestions to bridge any potential gaps

But are there any hidden traps that could negatively impact the outcomes and that need to be identified and kept on the radar? This is the question that we asked the students!

After considering the literature available, expert feedback, etc. They identified 3 main categories where AI could prove a formidable to the regulatory compliance field provided biases and impediments are adequately addressed: Efficiency, Accuracy, and Transparency.

 

Efficiency

 

 

 

Automating regulatory surveillance, intake and assessment can of course reduce manual efforts associated with these activities. Which is ultimately the goal. Furthermore, with more details on the regulation, its impacts, etc., technology can also improve human efficiency by providing subject matter experts with granular specifics at their fingertips. But organizations – and especially Legal and Compliance departments, should not forget that despite being automated, this activity still requires supervision. And therefore, professional competence in examining regulatory detail to ensure that the analysis is veridic.

In-house or externally contracted compliance expertise is therefore here to stay and should not be underestimated. Even if technology progresses.

 

Hopefully, human interpretation and error would be mitigated with adapted regulatory intelligence technology. But one of the findings of the reports is that “Due to a complicated regulatory detail, regulatory practice is principle-based, which leads to different interpretations among stakeholders, aggravated by the need for extensive documentation verification and the high number of false positives generated by monitoring systems”. If the technology lacks in-depth regulatory knowledge, then it will de facto affect the accuracy and applicability of the output. The choice of the underlying technology, including the LLM used of course, is therefore crucial for the success of the endeavour. A generic model, without the relevant body of knowledge will most likely provide misleading if not erroneous recommendations.

Accuracy

 

 

 

Transparency

 

 

We have all heard cases of AI hallucination – especially when it comes to Generative AI.

If it can be anecdotic when using GenAI for recreational purposes, for audits by regulatory bodies for instance, traceability of the sources used (guidelines, legislations, citations, etc.) will be a foundational requirement to ensure that there hasn’t been any fabricated response. Without it, trust will be impaired and so will Regulatory Intelligence initiatives.

Since these students followed the MSc Digital Business Innovation program, they also applied an “IT-lens” to their research. And one of the very last recommendations they made related to the IT landscape itself.

As per their feedback, recent studies have shown that AI can speed up compliance by eliminating the need for many of those manual processes that are generally slow and error prone. And this is great of course… Provided all systems are integrated. One of the main hurdles flagged in addition to the 3 main categories above resides in gaps in the integration between new technologies and long-standing legacy systems where a lot of the data resides. Which can prove cumbersome – and expensive if not included in the design of the initiative at its inception.

All in all: AI supporting compliance departments represents a great opportunity, but there are potential obstacles that need to be considered from the get-go to ensure a successful – and trustworthy, approach to Regulatory Intelligence powered by AI.

I hope this blog has given you a glimpse of what to look out for!

I would like to take this opportunity to sincerely thank Kainat Zanab, Kantida Apiraksattayakul, and Nikitha Keerthana Suthram Rajesh from Lancaster University Management School who have shared their great findings.

I look forward to reading your thoughts and comments either on this blog or on Twitter @TFrenehard

entry.title: Re: Architecting Email Approval without My Inbox for SAP Build Process Automation Workflows: Part 1
entry.content:
Hi @MustafaBensan ,

Thank you for your comments. It’s great to see that you liked our idea. For the productive version of the customer enhancements, we need to consider all validations and adopt a more modular approach. Given that this is a technical blog post, we can outline an end-to-end technical approach that is highly scalable with various combinations, as detailed below:

  1. Decoupling Email Notification Generation:
    This process is already decoupled from the triggered workflow. We have created a real-time CAP service that generates the request number and integrates with the email step. Thus, this CAP service is effectively decoupled from the triggering workflow.

  2. Desktop Agent Dependency:
    We have multiple options:
    a) A background scheduled CAP app that eventually triggers a process (SAP BPA)
    b) A desktop agent
    The decision can be based on factors such as the virtual machine environment. Therefore, the desktop agent is not the only option. For completeness, we have demonstrated one end-to-end process in the blog, showcasing complex handling via native SAP BPA.

  3. Email Validation and Security:
    This has already been addressed. Please refer to blog number 3 for details.

  4. Improving the Approver User Experience:
    The solution is as scalable as any other SAP BPA components and can be enhanced on a case-by-case basis.

Please feel free to connect in case needs any clarification.

Regards,
Mohit Bansal

entry.title: Re: Automating Multi-Sheet Excel Report Creation and Emailing in SAP ABAP
entry.content: Thanks. Just one remark about writing a "binary" file to the application server, if you have an XSTRING variable (Excel_As_Xstring), you may directly TRANSFER it once, you don't need to use the internal table. In your case, you already had the internal table (used by BCS) so it's harmless, but many people think that an internal table is needed to transfer an XSTRING variable to the application server and do this useless conversion.

entry.title: Re: How to add vairables to give results from last month,...
entry.content:
Hi Neilos,

try this

 

/**SELECT FROM [PCH1] T0 **/
DECLARE @Month AS INT
/* WHERE */
SET @Month = /* T0.Quantity */ [%0]

SELECT 
OPCH.DocNum, OPCH.DocDate, OPCH.CardCode, OPCH.CardName, OPCH.DocTotal, OPCH.VatSum, (OPCH.DocTotal-  OPCH.VatSum) as 'Nett' 

FROM OPCH OPCH 

WHERE 
OPCH.U_CDL ='y' 
and 
OPCH.DocDate >= DATEADD(MONTH, DATEDIFF(MONTH, 0, GETDATE()) - @Month, 0) 
and 
OPCH.DocDate <= DATEADD(MONTH, DATEDIFF(MONTH, - @Month, GETDATE()) - @Month, - @Month)
 
order by OPCH.DocNum asc

 

regards Lothar

entry.title: Re: Is Hungarian Notation as bad as "everyone" says it is? They are wrong!!! (oops clickba
entry.content:
No other language has this thing going on for him.

Even SAP are getting rid off it.

Properly named variables remove the need of a giving them a label whether they are global or local, table or not.

Learning to write code that follows some of the good practices ( SOLID for example ) will show you that you don't need millions of variables. I can't remember writing code with more than 3 to 4 instance variables per class (object). This reduces the complexity of the code and also removes the need to name them with this god awful naming convention. 

You can also have a look at Clean ABAP section about notations.

https://github.com/SAP/styleguides/blob/main/clean-abap/sub-sections/AvoidEncodings.md

entry.title: Re: SAP BW 7.5 migration to new box
entry.content:
Hi Ratish,

1. Question numbers 1 & 4 cannot migrate from the current version of NW 7.0 to new hardware NW 7.5.

2. You have to upgrade the existing SAP NW 7.0 to 7.5, to do this, you must go through the SAP Upgrade guide based on your OS & DB. 

3. During the upgrade process you have to check the OS / DB supported version.

Regards

SS

entry.title: Re: Configuration Transport Center: New way to view, download and transport configurations
entry.content:
Hi @hariprasadgud 

Thanks again for your reply, this time on my question on promoting a bundle.

I have raised a second question on that same day so maybe it was overlooked. Hence I raise it again: 

Would it be possible to split permissions between creating and updating a bundle and the actual transport of a bundle? Our idea on the process is that we want it to be possible for all Super Admins to work on bundles, but only 1 person to actually be charge of the transport to another instances. The idea behind it is to keep in control of what is transported and in what shape.

I have tried different combinations of settings but so far, I did not manage to separate the permission to transport from the permission to add to a bundle. Should it be possible yet?

If not possible, I'd like to raise an enhancement request. Can you please confirm?

Thank you,
Roos🌹

entry.title: Is the version of the Diagnostics Agent only dependent on the version of Solution Manager?
entry.content:
In case of following situation, does Diagnostics Agent 7.53 works normally?
・Instance which will be monitored by Solution manager：SAP Netweaver 7.3
・Solution manager 7.2
According to the Note2253383, Diagnostics Agent 7.53 is compatible with Solution manager 7.2.
However, there is no statement about the target host version, so is it correct that the version of the Diagnostics Agent only dependent on the version of Solution Manager?

Question and Answer:
Q: Please categorize your issue by choosing the option below that best fits your scenario.
A: None of the above

Q: What is the Release, OS, DB version of the affected system?
A:


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Is the version of the Diagnostics Agent only dependen...
entry.content:
Dear customer,

Greetings from SAP Support.

The Diagnostics Agent installation file is OS-independent and the version should be chosen depending on the Solution Manager version being connected to. 

Further details could be found in SAP Note 2253383 - Diagnostics Agent - SWPM Archive-Based Installation

Best regards,

SAP Support

entry.title: Re: Creation Large amount of Meter Reading Orders via ISU_S_METERREAD_CREATE | MSG EL422
entry.content:
Hello ud39,

the BAPI is the official supported way to upload meter readings into the system. FM ISU_S_METERREAD_CREATE is not released and therefore SAP will not guarantee that it is working in customer own coding.

Best regards,
Katrin
SAP Support

entry.title: How to assign multiple batches to one SFC in discrete industry
entry.content:
Dear Experts,

I would like to know how to achieve the relation among Order, SFC, and Batch below.

I've heard that :
1. One order can be executed with several batches (0..m).
2. One batch can be produced via several SFCs (0..n).
3. One SFC can lead to several batches (0..m).
4. Batches can be used across orders also

I'm not sure how to achieve each cases in DM.

Best regards,
Koichi Okamoto

entry.title: Loading C4C master data into BW/4HANA
entry.content:
Hi all,

I've noticed that in many cases, codes in C4C tables (could be any table: e.g. Sales Orders, Surveys, Visits) manage the master data through a separate file which can be downloaded and will usually include the code, description and language key:

Is there a way to connect directly to these tables/files/codes rather than downloading them and managing the master date through flat files?

I did raise an OSS as well to SAP and the official answer was that there is no direct proven method how to do it but to explore the  PDI (SDK) options which I'm about to explore. 

Any recommendations based on your experience?

Regards

Yoav

entry.title: SAP Build work zone, SAPUI5 app, controller not executing
entry.content:
Hi Folks,

I need your expert advice for an issue that I am facing when I deployed a simple UI5 application to cloud foundry.

I have created a simple UI5 which has a button and a label element, on press of which a function is called which displays a MessageBox. 

In view controller, I have added a MessageBox in init, just to verify is init is being called.

When I run the app with no-flp and sandbox, it runs perfectly fine.

When I deployed the app to cf , it is working fine when I open the app from HTML5 applications(I added FLP config while creating project).

Now when i ran the same app on Build Work Zone, the View controller is not loaded. The message box in init as well as button event is not displayed.  I observed that when I inspect the app, the controllers are not loaded in sources tab.

I need your advice for this issue.

entry.title: SAP B1 HANA - Catalina.out full of WARN/ERROR msgs
entry.content:
Hello all, we just discovered that the "catalina.out" log file handled by tomcat on the SAP HANA Server for SAP Business One have been growing slowly over a few months. Last week we discovered the log file was about 900GB. 

After cleaning this file and waiting for it to fill again with some data, we discovered that almost every second these errors are written in the file:

 

2024-12-02 07:47:47 ERROR [https-jsse-nio-40000-exec-52] com.sap.b1.sdk.oidc.core.handler.OAuth2Handler - not a valid token
io.jsonwebtoken.ExpiredJwtException: JWT expired at 2024-11-20T14:05:15Z. Current time: 2024-12-02T06:47:47Z, a difference of 1010552043 milliseconds.  Allowed clock skew: 0 milliseconds.
	at io.jsonwebtoken.impl.DefaultJwtParser.parse(DefaultJwtParser.java:427)
	at io.jsonwebtoken.impl.ImmutableJwtParser.parse(ImmutableJwtParser.java:148)
	at com.sap.b1.sdk.oidc.core.handler.OAuth2Handler.validateAccessToken(OAuth2Handler.java:174)
	at com.sap.b1.sdk.oidc.core.handler.OAuth2Handler.validateAccessToken(OAuth2Handler.java:218)
	at com.sap.b1.sdk.oidc.web.filter.OAuth2TokenFilter.parseAndValidate(OAuth2TokenFilter.java:183)
	at com.sap.b1.sdk.oidc.web.filter.OAuth2TokenFilter.handleAuthorizationHeader(OAuth2TokenFilter.java:125)
	at com.sap.b1.sdk.oidc.web.filter.OAuth2TokenFilter.doFilter(OAuth2TokenFilter.java:67)
	at org.apache.catalina.core.ApplicationFilterChain.internalDoFilter(ApplicationFilterChain.java:181)
	at org.apache.catalina.core.ApplicationFilterChain.doFilter(ApplicationFilterChain.java:156)
	at org.apache.catalina.core.StandardWrapperValve.invoke(StandardWrapperValve.java:168)
	at org.apache.catalina.core.StandardContextValve.invoke(StandardContextValve.java:90)
	at org.apache.catalina.authenticator.AuthenticatorBase.invoke(AuthenticatorBase.java:598)
	at org.apache.catalina.core.StandardHostValve.invoke(StandardHostValve.java:130)
	at org.apache.catalina.valves.ErrorReportValve.invoke(ErrorReportValve.java:93)
	at org.apache.catalina.core.StandardEngineValve.invoke(StandardEngineValve.java:74)
	at org.apache.catalina.connector.CoyoteAdapter.service(CoyoteAdapter.java:342)
	at org.apache.coyote.http11.Http11Processor.service(Http11Processor.java:617)
	at org.apache.coyote.AbstractProcessorLight.process(AbstractProcessorLight.java:63)
	at org.apache.coyote.AbstractProtocol$ConnectionHandler.process(AbstractProtocol.java:934)
	at org.apache.tomcat.util.net.NioEndpoint$SocketProcessor.doRun(NioEndpoint.java:1698)
	at org.apache.tomcat.util.net.SocketProcessorBase.run(SocketProcessorBase.java:52)
	at org.apache.tomcat.util.threads.ThreadPoolExecutor.runWorker(ThreadPoolExecutor.java:1191)
	at org.apache.tomcat.util.threads.ThreadPoolExecutor$Worker.run(ThreadPoolExecutor.java:659)
	at org.apache.tomcat.util.threads.TaskThread$WrappingRunnable.run(TaskThread.java:61)
	at java.lang.Thread.run(Thread.java:838)
2024-12-02 07:48:01 WARN [https-jsse-nio-40000-exec-71] com.sap.b1.sdk.oidc.web.filter.OAuth2TokenFilter - check authorization header error
com.sap.b1.sdk.oidc.core.exception.B1SDKNotAuthenticatedException: invalid access token
	at com.sap.b1.sdk.oidc.web.filter.OAuth2TokenFilter.parseAndValidate(OAuth2TokenFilter.java:185)
	at com.sap.b1.sdk.oidc.web.filter.OAuth2TokenFilter.handleAuthorizationHeader(OAuth2TokenFilter.java:125)
	at com.sap.b1.sdk.oidc.web.filter.OAuth2TokenFilter.doFilter(OAuth2TokenFilter.java:67)
	at org.apache.catalina.core.ApplicationFilterChain.internalDoFilter(ApplicationFilterChain.java:181)
	at org.apache.catalina.core.ApplicationFilterChain.doFilter(ApplicationFilterChain.java:156)
	at org.apache.catalina.core.StandardWrapperValve.invoke(StandardWrapperValve.java:168)
	at org.apache.catalina.core.StandardContextValve.invoke(StandardContextValve.java:90)
	at org.apache.catalina.authenticator.AuthenticatorBase.invoke(AuthenticatorBase.java:598)
	at org.apache.catalina.core.StandardHostValve.invoke(StandardHostValve.java:130)
	at org.apache.catalina.valves.ErrorReportValve.invoke(ErrorReportValve.java:93)
	at org.apache.catalina.core.StandardEngineValve.invoke(StandardEngineValve.java:74)
	at org.apache.catalina.connector.CoyoteAdapter.service(CoyoteAdapter.java:342)
	at org.apache.coyote.http11.Http11Processor.service(Http11Processor.java:617)
	at org.apache.coyote.AbstractProcessorLight.process(AbstractProcessorLight.java:63)
	at org.apache.coyote.AbstractProtocol$ConnectionHandler.process(AbstractProtocol.java:934)
	at org.apache.tomcat.util.net.NioEndpoint$SocketProcessor.doRun(NioEndpoint.java:1698)
	at org.apache.tomcat.util.net.SocketProcessorBase.run(SocketProcessorBase.java:52)

	at org.apache.tomcat.util.threads.ThreadPoolExecutor$Worker.run(ThreadPoolExecutor.java:659)
	at org.apache.tomcat.util.threads.TaskThread$WrappingRunnable.run(TaskThread.java:61)
	at java.lang.Thread.run(Thread.java:838)

 

I was not able to find any documentation or SAP note about this subject. Given the fact that it is writting these ERROR/WARN every seconds, I would like to know if this is normal or not and if it's not normal, how to correct this issue? 

File location is here: /hana/usr/sap/SAPBusinessOne/Common/tomcat/logs

Since we discovered this, at the moment we "clean" this file using the following cmd every day with cron: 

cat /dev/null > /hana/usr/sap/SAPBusinessOne/Common/tomcat/logs/catalina.out

Thank you.

Best regards

entry.title: Key User Custom Fields in SAP S/4HANA SAP GUI UIs: How to control field properties (mandatory,...)
entry.content:
Key User Custom Fields in SAP S/4HANA SAP GUI UIs: How to control field properties like mandatory, read-only, and visibility

Do you want to influence the field properties of your key user custom fields in SAP GUI UIs (note that we subsequently use the term custom field instead of key user custom field)? Do you want to configure them as mandatory or read-only or invisible, even dynamically, based on specific business data?

Please note that there is another blog post that introduces the topic of field control for custom fields. It also guides you on how to find out which of these detail blogs you should look at in your specific scenario.
The reason we have several detail blogs is that what you have to do in order to implement field control for custom fields heavily depends on the standard UIs’ implementation technology.
When you already know that in your scenario this technology is SAP GUI, then this blog post is just for you.

This blog post covers the following topics:

  • How to check if your SAP GUI UI supports key user custom fields
  • Whether field control is available for those fields and, if so:
    • How to set your custom fields mandatory or read-only in a static or dynamic way
    • How to set your custom field visible or invisible in a dynamic way

Please note that this blog doesn’t only address SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, but also SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition and SAP S/4HANA On-Premise.

Let’s assume we want to add custom field to the Change Sales Orders - VA02 app. When you open the app details within the Fiori Apps Library, you’ll find the Application Type field. For this app (VA02) the field shows SAP GUI, which means that it’s a SAP GUI UI.
In order to find out whether this SAP GUI UI supports custom fields, you can have a look at the txt file representing a table attached to this blog (FieldControlSupportByGUITransaction.txt - the txt file is a table where the entries within one row are separated by a tab), which shows all SAP GUI transactions that support custom fields extensibility (this table is taken from SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition (2408) and SAP S/4HANA On-Premise 2023). One way to work with this txt file is to open it in excel. The first column shows the Fiori ID of the SAP GUI transaction (in almost all cases identical with the transaction code). The second column shows the SAP GUI context. The third column shows the business context. The fourth column shows whether field control is supported for the given transaction/SAP GUI context/business context, and the fifth column shows the name of the field control BAdI in case it is supported.
A small number of ABAP Web Dynpro-based UIs use exactly the same custom field method to support custom field extensibility. These are also listed in the attached file, and what is described in this blog also applies to them.  
As this table also shows which business contexts are exposed by which SAP GUI contexts, it helps you to verify if the SAP GUI transaction in your situation supports custom fields, for which business context you need to create the field, and which SAP GUI context you have to enable to make it visible in the UI.

For our example for transaction VA02 (Fiori ID VA02) there are two SAP GUI contexts listed:

  1. SD_SALESDOC_GUI: this exposes business context SD_SALESDOC
  2. SD_SALESDOCITEM_GUI: this exposes business context SD_SALESDOCITEM

In the attached file, you can see whether you can implement field control for custom fields enabled for the respective SAP GUI context (we’ll take SD_SALESDOC_GUI as an example).
If this is the case, the column Supports FieldControlSupported shows true. In the column BAdI Name you can see the name of the Cloud BAdI which you must implement get field control for your respective custom field.
In case it turns out that the SAP GUI transaction in your scenario is not custom field-enabled at all, or field control is not supported for the respective SAP GUI context, create a request via the customer influence channel.

You can enable a SAP GUI context for a given custom field by opening the details screen of the custom field in the Custom Fields Fiori app. Within the “User Interfaces” tab,  select “SAP GUI”.
The description of the SAP GUI context describes in almost all cases on which SAP GUI UI this SAP GUI context is exposed. Please note that one SAP GUI context can be exposed in multiple transactions.
In the Excel table attached to this blog, you can check which business context is exposed in which SAP GUI transaction and which respective SAP GUI context you should enable there for the given custom field.
By enabling a SAP GUI context in Fiori app Custom Fields a custom field automatically appears in an UI section of the corresponding transaction. If you want to know how to place a custom field exactly where you want it to be in your SAP GUI transaction, read this blog: . https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-blogs-by-sap/sap-screen-personas-how-to-position-key-user-custom-fields-exactly-where/ba-p/13553723

In some SAP GUI transactions which expose SAP GUI contexts, a BAdI is provided, allowing you to influence the properties of the custom fields dynamically: read-only, mandatory, visible. You can check the Excel table at the end of this blog to find out which of the SAP GUI transactions supports this.

SAP application development uses the BTP ABAP API class CL_CFD_SAP_GUI_CONTEXT_API to support dynamic field control for their SAP GUI contexts. In almost all BAdIs that SAP provides to allow to implement field control for custom fields, the signature of the respective BAdI method is a changing table which has the following columns:

field_name TYPE c LENGTH 30
read_only  TYPE abap_bool.
invisible  TYPE abap_bool.
mandatory  TYPE abap_bool.

By populating this changing table, you can influence the behavior of the respective custom field by setting the read-only/invisible/mandatory flag for the field with the respective field name. Mind that you should not add entries, but only change the property values, as for each custom field in the respective SAP GUI context, a record is already provided.  

Let’s look at a concrete example:
As a prerequisite, we created the custom field “ContractLink” of type “Web Address” using the Custom Fields app on business context SD_SALESDOC and made it visible in the SAP GUI UI “Sales - Sales Order Processing: Change Sales Orders (VA02)” (Fiori Apps Library link). This blog post gives you more information about placing the custom field exactly where you want it to be in your SAP GUI transaction.

When you want to follow the implementation steps described in this blog on your own in an SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition system, please make sure that at least the following business catalogs are assigned to your user: SAP_SD_BC_SO_PROC_MC, SAP_CORE_BC_EXT_FLD, SAP_CORE_BC_EXT_BLE.

In the Custom Fields app, the field looks like this:

ContractLink custom field in the Custom Fields app

In transaction VA02 (Change Sales Orders) the field looks like this (you can navigate to this screen via Goto --> Header --> Additional Data --> Custom Fields):

ContractLink custom field in VA02 (Change Sales Orders)

Let’s now assume we’d like to make the custom field ContractLink mandatory within the VA02 UI.
The first step is to find a Cloud BAdI (Business Add-In) that allows us to influence the field control information for custom fields in transaction VA02. We can do that by navigating to the Custom Logic Fiori app. In the app, we select Create on the top right corner of the screen.
Within the Business Context drop-down of the search bar, we enter the business context of our field Sales: Business Context. This results in the following list of Cloud BAdIs:

BAdI selection

There, we select the BAdI Custom Field Properties in Sales Document Header as this is the one allowing us to control the field properties of our custom fields.

On the bottom on the left side, we select “Step 2”. Then, we can provide an implementation description and an implementation ID:

Providing implementation ID and description in the Custom Logic app

Then, we select Review and then Create. With that, the BAdI implementation has been created. Before we can create our ABAP coding, we have to publish this BAdI implementation. To do that, we select Publish.
After this has been done, we can click Open Code Editor and with this, we’re navigated to the ABAP editor allowing us to implement our logic.

Implementing the field control logic in the BAdI

On the right side of the editor, we see the signature of the BAdI method. We see our ContractLink field with its technical name YY1_CONTRACTLINK_SDH within the importing parameters.
The next step is to enter our code. We want to make the field mandatory in an unconditional way. We do so by entering the code lines:

read table field_properties with table key field_name = 'YY1_CONTRACTLINK_SDH' reference into data(lr_contractlink_field).
if sy-subrc = 0.
   lr_contractlink_field->mandatory = 'X'.
endif.

Coding of the field control logic in the BAdI

The next step is to validate that

  1. the field is shown as mandatory on the UI
  2. you get an error message in case you don’t give a value for this field and select Save

When you now reopen VA02, we see that the red star for the mandatory field is provided:

ContractLink field shown as mandatory

When you clear the field and try to save it, you immediately get an error:

ContractLink  field mandatory check

It’s exactly the same for the other properties like invisible or read-only. Just set the flag for the respective custom field in the BAdI, and it'll be applied in the UI.

Please note that the signature of the BAdI might be different in other application areas and other SAP GUI UIs. In some cases, it might be necessary to implement two BAdIs: one that takes care that the field is marked as mandatory, and one that provides the check whether the field really is populated. In case of transaction VA02, this is both done via BAdI Custom Field Properties in Sales Document Header.

Now, you are done! Congratulations!
With this, you’re now equipped to

  • find out whether the SAP GUI transaction at hand supports custom fields and
  • if so, whether it supports field control for custom fields and
  • if so, how you can find out the BAdI(s) you must implement to realize the respective field control and
  • you know how to code your field control logic.

Feedback, Comments, or Questions:

You’re cordially invited to provide them in the comments section below. Please follow our profiles to get updates when we publish more posts on these topics.
Feel invited to post and answer questions here (https://answers.sap.com/tags/270c4f37-c335-46e1-bfad-a256637d5e26) and read other posts on the topic (https://blogs.sap.com/tags/270c4f37-c335-46e1-bfad-a256637d5e26/).

Other related blog posts:

  1. Custom Fields in S/4HANA: How to control field properties like mandatory, read-only, and visibility
  2. Key User Custom Fields in Fiori UIs based on SEGW OData Services: How to control field properties
  3. Field Control for Developer Extensibility Custom Fields
  4. Key User Custom Fields in Fiori UIs based on OData Services exposed with Service Binding
  5. Custom fields in S/4HANA: Key User versus Developer Extensibility
  6. SAP Screen Personas: How to position key user custom fields exactly where you want them to be

entry.title: Date range input control - SAC optimized Story
entry.content:
Hello, 
I'm asking again because my previous post seems to have missed.

entry.title: Re: How to add vairables to give results from last month,...
entry.content:
Hi,

fine that it works.

The comments are neccessary for B1. You choose a table and a field in it.

The trick here is that the Name shown in the parameter-question window is used as in the gui from B1.

With it you can declare a variable at the beginning if the query and not suing [%0] on different points.

Here some more examples

/*Buchungsdatum*/
/**SELECT FROM [OFPR] T0 **/
DECLARE @DocDateFrom AS Date
/* WHERE */
SET @DocDateFrom = /* T0.F_RefDate */ '[%0]'

/**SELECT FROM [OFPR] T1 **/
DECLARE @DocDateTo AS Date
/* WHERE */
SET @DocDateTo = /* T1.T_RefDate */ '[%1]'

-----
/*Fälligkeitsdatum*/
/**SELECT FROM [OFPR] T0 **/
DECLARE @DocDateFrom AS Date
/* WHERE */
SET @DocDateFrom = /* T0.F_DueDate */ '[%0]'

/**SELECT FROM [OFPR] T1 **/
DECLARE @DocDateTo AS Date
/* WHERE */
SET @DocDateTo = /* T1.T_DueDate */ '[%1]'

-----
/*Belegdatum*/
/**SELECT FROM [OFPR] T0 **/
DECLARE @DocDateFrom AS Date
/* WHERE */
SET @DocDateFrom = /* T0.F_TaxDate */ '[%0]'

/**SELECT FROM [OFPR] T1 **/
DECLARE @DocDateTo AS Date
/* WHERE */
SET @DocDateTo = /* T1.T_TaxDate */ '[%1]'

----------------------
/**SELECT FROM [OHEM] T0 **/
DECLARE @DocDateFrom AS Date
/* WHERE */
SET @DocDateFrom = /* T0.StartDate */ '[%0]'

/**SELECT FROM [OHEM] T1 **/
DECLARE @DocDateTo AS Date
/* WHERE */
SET @DocDateTo = /* T1.termDate */ '[%1]'

For us we made an UDT with UDF's only to Name the Parameters as we want, because the Name of the UDF's is shown in the window

regards Lothar

entry.title: Custom Fields in S/4HANA: How to control field properties like mandatory, read-only, and visibility
entry.content:
Custom Fields in S/4HANA: How to control field properties like mandatory, read-only, and visibility

Do you want to influence the field properties of your custom fields? Do you want to configure them as mandatory, or read-only, or invisible, even dynamically based on specific business data?

If your answer is yes, then this blog post is just for you.

Please note that this blog doesn't only address SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, but also SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition and SAP S/4HANA On-Premise.

This blog post covers the following topics:

  • How to set your custom fields mandatory or read-only in a static or dynamic way
  • How to set your custom field visible or invisible in a dynamic way

The answer to these questions differs significantly based on the type of custom field you have at hand:

  1. Key user custom fields: these are custom fields which have been created using the Custom Fields Fiori app
  2. Developer extensibility custom fields: these are custom fields which have been created using ABAP development tools for Eclipse (ADT), either manually, or with the extension fields wizard.

However, this isn't the only dimension that affects how you can implement field control. The second dimension to consider is the UI and OData technology SAP uses to implement the respective standard application.

Below, you can find links to blogs covering the different types of custom fields and applications. They also explain how to find out if field extensibility or field control is supported at all.

  1. Key User Custom Fields in Fiori UIs based on SEGW OData Services: How to control field properties
  2. Key User Custom Fields in SAP S/4HANA SAP GUI UIs: How to control field properties (mandatory,...)
  3. Field Control for Developer Extensibility Custom Fields
  4. Key User Custom Fields in Fiori UIs based on OData Services exposed with Service Binding

This blog is intended to help you find the category your specific scenario belongs to and which of the subsequent blogs you should follow.

The simplest way to start is to have a look in the Fiori Apps Library.
Let’s take the example app Manage Sales Orders - Version 2. When you open this link, you’ll find a field called Application Type within the app details start page. In the app, this field is labeled with Transactional (SAP Fiori elements). This shows that it’s not a SAP GUI user interface. Instead, it’s a Fiori application based on an OData service.
An example for Application Type SAP GUI UI is the app Change Sales Orders - VA02. Note that you can find the transaction code of your SAP GUI app in the field Transaction Code within the Fiori Apps Library documentation of the app (in this case, it’s VA02).

In case it’s a Fiori UI, the remaining question is now whether the underlying OData service is a SEGW-based service, or a service binding based OData service.
To find out, open the Fiori Apps Library documentation of the app. Select the IMPLEMENTATION INFORMATION tab and then click Configuration. You’ll find the name of the OData service(s) you’re looking for in the OData Service(s)/OData V4 Service Group(s) area. In our example, this is C_SALESORDERMANAGE_SRV. Now, open ADT (ABAP development tools for Eclipse). Within ADT, search for a service binding with exactly that OData service (in our case C_SALESORDERMANAGE_SRV). If you find it, this means that it’s a service binding or service definition-based OData service. If you don’t find the OData service in ADT it means that it’s a SEGW-based OData service.

Now you know which category your application belongs to and which blog you should have a look at to see whether field control for custom fields is supported, and how you can implement it. The following visualization shows the investigation steps described before.

 

Steps how to find out to which application type the SAP standard application belongs to

Please note that in one of the future releases, SAP will offer a generative AI-driven Extensibility AI Assistant which helps you find out whether a given app supports field extensibility, and whether field control is supported. It’ll also explain what you have to do in order to implement that.

Feedback, Comments, or Questions:

You’re cordially invited to provide feedback, comments, or questions in the comments section below. Please follow our profiles to get updates when we publish more posts on these topics.
Feel invited to post and answer questions here (https://answers.sap.com/tags/270c4f37-c335-46e1-bfad-a256637d5e26) and read other posts on the topic (https://blogs.sap.com/tags/270c4f37-c335-46e1-bfad-a256637d5e26/).

Other related blog posts:

  1. Key User Custom Fields in SAP S/4HANA SAP GUI UIs: How to control field properties (mandatory,...)
  2. Key User Custom Fields in Fiori UIs based on SEGW OData Services: How to control field properties
  3. Field Control for Developer Extensibility Custom Fields
  4. Key User Custom Fields in Fiori UIs based on OData Services exposed with Service Binding
  5. How to implement a custom field of type “Code List based on CDS view” with a developer extensibility custom entity:  https://blogs.sap.com/2022/07/26/sap-s-4hana-key-user-extensibility-powered-by-embedded-steampunk-custom-field-with-abap-implemented-value-help/
    Also: “How to implement a custom entity in the ABAP RESTful Programming Model using remote function modules”   
  6. How to configure the value help as a context-dependent value help: https://blogs.sap.com/2022/07/27/sap-s-4hana-key-user-extensibility-custom-field-with-abap-context-dependent-value-help/
  7. How to configure intent-based navigation on a key user custom field of type “Code List based on CDS View”: https://blogs.sap.com/2023/06/06/enrich-sap-s-4hana-key-user-custom-fields-with-intent-based-navigation/ 
    and in this case, how to easily find the fitting semantic object and semantic object parameter: Custom field type “Code list based on CDS view”: Finding/defining the right value help view and the right Semantic Object and Semantic Object Parameter for Intent-Based Navigation
  8. Custom fields in S/4HANA: Key User versus Developer Extensibility

entry.title: Key User Custom Fields in Fiori UIs based on SEGW OData Services: How to control field properties
entry.content:
Key User Custom Fields in SAP S/4HANA Fiori UIs based on SEGW OData Services: How to control field properties like mandatory, read-only, and visibility

 

Do you want to influence the field properties of your key user custom fields (note that we subsequently use the term custom field instead of key user custom field) in SAP standard UIs based on OData services built with SEGW (Gateway Service Builder)? Do you want to configure them as mandatory or read-only or invisible, even dynamically, based on specific business data? 

Please note that there is another blog post that introduces the topic of field control for custom fields. It also guides you on how to find out which of these detail blogs you should look at in your specific scenario.
The reason we have several detail blogs is that what you have to do in order to implement field control for custom fields heavily depends on the standard UIs’ implementation technology.
When you already know that in your scenario this technology is SEGW, then this blog post is just for you.

This blog post covers the following topics:

  • How you can find out whether the respective application supports custom fields,
  • and whether field control is supported in this case, and if so:
    • How to set your custom fields mandatory or read-only in a static or dynamic way
    • How to set your custom field visible or invisible in a dynamic way

Please note that this blog doesn’t only address SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, but also SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition and SAP S/4HANA On-Premise.

For some of these custom field-enabled OData service entities, SAP enabled dynamic custom field property control by providing a BAdI (Business Add-In) that you can implement to influence the value of the respective field control field. You can find out whether field control for custom fields is supported for your scenario for the respective OData service entity by having a look at the txt file attached to this blog. The txt file is basically a table where the entries within one row are separated by a tab. One way to work with this txt file is to open it in excel.

  • The first column shows the Fiori ID of the Fiori UI
  • The second column shows the name of the OData service (you find out how you can get the name of this OData service for the given Fiori app in this blog 
  • The third column shows the name of the OData service entity
  • The fourth column shows the name(s) of the exposed business context(s)
  • The fifth column shows  whether field control is supported or not
  • The last column shows the name of the BAdI that you have to implement to implement field control for your custom field

Please note that this table is based on SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2408 and SAP S/4HANA On-Premise 2023. Most of the entries are valid for both editions, but not all of them.
In case it turns out that the SEGW OData service-based Fiori UI in your scenario is not custom field-enabled at all, or field control is not supported for the respective SAP GUI context, please create a respective extensibility enabling request via the customer influence channel.

In the section above, the term 'field control field' has been introduced. You may wonder what a field control field is:
Custom fields can be propagated into all OData service entities which have been enabled for key user extensibility by SAP by using the Custom Fields Fiori app.
As soon as a custom field is propagated to an OData service entity, the additional property for the field itself is added. Additionally, a field control field is added, which is referenced by the field property. This field control property shows whether the field should be visible, mandatory, or read-only (you can find detailed information regarding this field control feature here: https://sap.github.io/odata-vocabularies/docs/v2-annotations.html#attribute-sapfield-control ).

The possible values for a field control property are listed here:

Value

Meaning

7

Mandatory - property must contain a value

3

Optional - property may contain a null value

1

Read-only - property cannot be changed

0

Hidden - property should not be visible on user interfaces

 Set the value to “7” to set the field to mandatory or “1” to set the field to read-only. As this is dynamic by nature, you can even set these values depending on the values of fields that are provided such as importing and changing data within the BAdI’s method signature.
Based on such a BAdI implementation, you can influence how and whether the field is rendered in a UI. In most cases, it’s sufficient to implement one BAdI to

  1. make sure that the field gets the red mandatory star on the top right corner of its label in the UI and
  2. to have a runtime check in place to check whether the value of a mandatory key user custom field really has been filled in

Please note that in some cases, application development may have separated both aspects into two different BAdIs. In this case, you have to implement both BAdIs.

The following example shows how to set a custom field to mandatory in case the respective OData service is implemented via SEGW and supports field control for custom fields:

As a prerequisite, we created the custom field JobTitle of type Text using the Custom Fields Fiori app on business context MKT_INTERACTION_CONTACT and made it visible in the Fiori app Contact Profile. The example is taken from SAP Hybris Marketing Cloud.

In the Custom Fields Fiori app, the field looks like this:

JobTitle  custom field in the Custom Fields Fiori app

In the Contact Profile app, the custom field looks like this:

 

JobTitle  custom field in the Contact Profile Fiori app

 Next, implement the field control logic to set the JobTitle custom field to mandatory in this UI. First, we have to search for the BAdI that we have to implement  to influence the field control logic of custom fields in the Contact Profile app. Follow the steps described previously using the txt file attached to this blog. If there is a field control BAdI available for your given OData service and business context, you’ll know that in your case, field control is supported.

Now, open the Custom Logic app (Fiori Apps Library link: https://fioriappslibrary.hana.ondemand.com/sap/fix/externalViewer/#/detail/Apps('F6957')/S32) .
In the app, select Create to create a new implementation. Then, search the BAdI in the search field. The BAdI with the description “Field Control of Custom Fields in Contact UI” is found. This is exactly the one we have to implement (see screenshot). In case you don’t have the table at hand that we’ve mentioned before, you can also search for the BAdI using the respective business context.

Select the BAdI and choose “Step 2”.

BAdI selection in the  Custom Logic app

In the next step, enter the implementation ID and the implementation description (see screenshot). Then, click  Review.

Providing implementation ID and description in the  Custom Logic app

On the review screen, you can check what you’ve entered so far. When you’re done, select Create (see screenshot).

BAdI creation in the Custom Logic app

Publish the implementation by clicking Publish. Then, choose Open Code Editor to open the Custom Logic editor (see screenshot).

Implementing the field control logic in the BAdI

Select the changing parameters. Within the FIELDCONTROL_VALUES_CONTACT section, you’ll find the field control field of the JobTitle field, which is the field with the name YY1_JOBTITLE_MPSF. Note that the “F” at the end of the field name shows that this is a field control field that controls the behavior of the field with the field name YY1_JOBTITLE_MPS.
As shown in the screenshot, assign the value “7” (which is the value for mandatory) to this field control field and publish the change. Then, you’re done.

When you open the Contact Profile app UI once again, you’ll see that there is a red star on the top right of the label of the JobTitle field (see screenshot).

JobTitle field shown as mandatory

When you try to save the contact with an empty JobTitle field, you’ll get an error message explaining that saving is not possible as long as the field doesn’t have a value (see screenshot).

JobTitle field mandatory check

For those of you who are interested in more technical details, you can have a look into the metadata of the OData service (URL: /sap/opu/odata/SAP/CUAN_CONTACT_SRV/$metadata). There, you’ll find the following two properties for the EntityType Contact within the entities OData metadata:

  1. <Property Name="YY1_JobTitle_ENH" Type="Edm.String" MaxLength="20" sap:field-control="YY1_JobTitle_ENHF" sap:label="JobTitle" sap:filterable="false" sap:is-extension-field="true"/>
  2. <Property Name="YY1_JobTitle_ENHF" Type="Edm.Byte" sap:visible="false" sap:label="UI Field Control" sap:creatable="false" sap:updatable="false" sap:filterable="false" sap:is-extension-field="true"/>

The first property is the one for the JobTitle custom field itself. You’ll notice that the reference sap:field-control="YY1_JobTitle_ENHF" points to the second one, which is the property for the field control field of the JobTitle custom field.

For every custom field exposed in a SEGW-built OData service entity, there is a field control field.

Now, you are done! Congratulations!
With this, you’re now equipped to

  • find out whether the Fiori app based on a SEGW-built OData service supports custom fields, and
  • if so, whether it supports field control for custom fields and
  • if so, how you can find the BAdI(s) you must implement to realize the respective field control and code your field control logic.

Feedback, Comments, or Questions:

You’re cordially invited to provide them in the comments section below. Please follow our profiles to get updates when we publish more posts on these topics.
Feel invited to post and answer questions here (https://answers.sap.com/tags/270c4f37-c335-46e1-bfad-a256637d5e26) and read other posts on the topic (https://blogs.sap.com/tags/270c4f37-c335-46e1-bfad-a256637d5e26/).

Other related blog posts:

  1. Custom Fields in S/4HANA: How to control field properties like mandatory, read-only, and visibility
  2. Key User Custom Fields in SAP S/4HANA SAP GUI UIs: How to control field properties (mandatory,...)
  3. Field Control for Developer Extensibility Custom Fields
  4. Key User Custom Fields in Fiori UIs based on OData Services exposed with Service Binding
  5. Custom fields in S/4HANA: Key User versus Developer Extensibility
  6. SAP Screen Personas: How to position key user custom fields exactly where you want them to be

entry.title: will the custom field not work with the "GM_MLT_BASED_RATING_CALCULATOR"?
entry.content:
We have configured a goal plan with the "GM_MLT_BASED_RATING_CALCULATOR." With this setup, we are able to populate the manager score (standard rating field), but for the employee, we have created a custom field, and the score is not auto-populating in the goal plan. Our question is: will the custom field not work with the "GM_MLT_BASED_RATING_CALCULATOR"? If the answer is no, we understand, but if yes, we would like to explore building a similar setup for the employee. This would allow the form to include the "get feedback" option, so the feedback provider’s rating can also be captured for reference."

Please let us know the solution for this.

entry.title: Re: Vendor: Withholding Tax NOT extended in new company code
entry.content: You could add a customer FM to an event (tr. BUS7) to add/trigger the copy in your own code, look for event DSAVE for example.

entry.title: Is it possible to have two different settings within a single goal plan?
entry.content: We have a goal plan that includes the "GM_MLT_BASED_RATING_CALCULATOR," which generates scores based on the MLT table. The MLT table is manually set by the employee during the goal-setting stage. The current requirement is to have some goals with a fixed MLT table that should be pre-populated. Is it possible to have two different settings within a single goal plan? If so, can we configure the goal plan to include a pre-set MLT table without any predefined range or formula, while still allowing the employee to make edits if necessary?

entry.title: Re: Why is Hungarian Notation Harmful?
entry.content: I cannot believe, in our team, how much time has been wasted enforcing stupid HN. At the end of the day, SAP is to blame, as the naming of variables this way was promoted in their documentation. But it adds zero value. The "I need to see what kind of variable this is" argument holds no water. If you struggle to read or understand code because it does not have HN then, I am sorry, the problem is not with the code, buddy.
